headline: MSFT vs. PLTR vs. CRWD: Down 25% or More, Which Beaten-Down Tech Stock Is the Best Buy Now?
summary: After years of leading the market higher, large-cap technology stocks have hit a speed bump in 2026. Concerns around artificial intelligence (AI) disruption, geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, and a softer macro backdrop have weighed on the sector. That pullback has created an opportunity to revisit some of the market’s top names at more reasonable valuations. headline: Morning Movers: Sysco dips following deal to acquire Jetro Restaurant Depot
summary: Stock futures are modestly higher to start the week, indicating a potential rebound at the open following five consecutive weeks of declines across major indexes. Markets are stabilizing slightly after last week’s sustained selloff, but the broader tone remains cautious as energy prices and geopolitical risk continue to dominate. headline: CrowdStrike and HCLTech Expand Strategic Partnership with AI-Powered Continuous Threat Exposure Management Services
summary: AUSTIN, Texas & NEW YORK & NOIDA, India, March 31, 2026--CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) and HCLTech today announced an expansion of their strategic partnership with the launch of Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) services. headline: What Happens When AI Agents Go Rogue?
summary: Spend enough time around cybersecurity types, and questions like the one above might feel like lighthearted chitchat.  The annual confab is one of the largest gatherings of cybersecurity professionals, and the timing of this year’s gathering proved particularly apt.  Near the end of the week, an internal document from Anthropic leaked to the public, about a new AI model so powerful that it poses significant cybersecurity risks because of its potential to become a tool for hackers.

content.title: What Happens When AI Agents Go Rogue?
content.summary: Spend enough time around cybersecurity types, and questions like the one above might feel like lighthearted chitchat.  The annual confab is one of the largest gatherings of cybersecurity professionals, and the timing of this year’s gathering proved particularly apt.  Near the end of the week, an internal document from Anthropic leaked to the public, about a new AI model so powerful that it poses significant cybersecurity risks because of its potential to become a tool for hackers.
